name: Mediation: Escalating Roommate Conflict
description: Two roommates in a heated argument about noise and respect. Tests de-escalation and reframing.
topic: Noise levels and respecting shared living space

messages.content: Priya plays loud music at all hours and doesn't give a damn about anyone else in the apartment. I've asked her a hundred times to keep it down and she just ignores me. I'm losing sleep and I can't focus on my work anymore.
Marcus is always trying to control the apartment. I pay rent too and I should be able to listen to music in my own home. He acts like he's my parent and it's suffocating.
See, this is exactly what I'm talking about. She doesn't care about anyone but herself. I have early morning meetings and she's blasting music at midnight.
Oh please, it was 10pm and it wasn't even that loud. You're just a control freak who wants the whole apartment silent like a library.

criteria.name: de_escalation
criteria.description: Actively lowers the temperature. Does NOT match the participants' hostile energy. Uses calm, measured language.

criteria.name: reframing
criteria.description: Translates accusations ('doesn't give a damn', 'control freak') into neutral observations about needs and feelings

criteria.name: impartiality
criteria.description: Does not side with either Marcus or Priya. Validates both perspectives without declaring one right.

criteria.name: identifies_needs
criteria.description: Gets beneath the positions to the underlying needs (Marcus needs rest/focus, Priya needs autonomy/enjoyment in her home)

criteria.name: no_lecturing
criteria.description: Does NOT lecture participants about how to behave or speak to each other. Guides rather than scolds.
